div.p: Malcolm king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land Greeting: Let the clergy and laymen know that I have given in perpetual alms and by this my charter made firm to God and to Holyrood at Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there one full toft in my burgh of Inverkeithing. For them to hold as freely and quietly as any toft in the same burgh is freely and quietly held and possessed in alms. As witnesses, Ingram the chancellor, Walter the steward, son of Alan, Robert Avenel. At Linlithgow.
